rejected_candidates.label: llm_sql_direct
rejected_candidates.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.doctor_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
WHERE p.patient_id IN (
    SELECT patient_id
    FROM appointments
    JOIN doctors ON appointments.doctor_id = doctors.doctor_id
    GROUP BY patient_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT doctors.specialty) > 1
)
AND i.total_amount > (
    SELECT AVG(i2.total_amount)
    FROM invoices i2
    JOIN appointments a2 ON i2.appointment_id = a2.appointment_id
    JOIN patients p2 ON a2.patient_id = p2.patient_id
    WHERE p2.insurance_provider = p.insurance_provider
)
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name

semantic_checklist.comparison_logic: Patient's total invoiced amount must be greater than the average total amount of all patients sharing the same insurance provider

generated_sql.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.doctor_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
WHERE p.insurance_provider = (
    SELECT p2.insurance_provider
    FROM patients p2
    WHERE p2.patient_id = p.patient_id
)
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT d.specialty) > 1
   AND SUM(i.total_amount) > (
       SELECT AVG(patient_total)
       FROM (
           SELECT p3.insurance_provider, SUM(i2.total_amount) AS patient_total
           FROM patients p3
           JOIN appointments a3 ON p3.patient_id = a3.patient_id
           JOIN invoices i2 ON a3.appointment_id = i2.appointment_id
           GROUP BY p3.patient_id
       ) sub
       WHERE sub.insurance_provider = p.insurance_provider
   )

rejected_candidates.label: llm_sql_direct
rejected_candidates.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name, p.insurance_provider
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
WHERE i.payment_status IN ('unpaid', 'partial')
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name, p.insurance_provider
HAVING SUM(i.total_amount - i.insurance_paid) > (
    SELECT AVG(outstanding_balance)
    FROM (
        SELECT p2.insurance_provider, SUM(i2.total_amount - i2.insurance_paid) AS outstanding_balance
        FROM patients p2
        JOIN appointments a2 ON p2.patient_id = a2.patient_id
        JOIN invoices i2 ON a2.appointment_id = i2.appointment_id
        WHERE i2.payment_status IN ('unpaid', 'partial')
        GROUP BY p2.patient_id, p2.insurance_provider
    ) sub
    WHERE sub.insurance_provider = p.insurance_provider
)
reasons: SQL uses unmentioned table 'invoices' while the question names specific tables ['patients']
possible join fanout aggregate inflation

semantic_checklist.comparison_logic: Patient's outstanding balance must be greater than the average outstanding balance of patients with the same insurance provider

generated_sql.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name, p.insurance_provider
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
WHERE i.payment_status IN ('unpaid', 'partial')
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name, p.insurance_provider
HAVING SUM(i.total_amount - i.insurance_paid) > (
    SELECT AVG(outstanding)
    FROM (
        SELECT p2.insurance_provider, SUM(i2.total_amount - i2.insurance_paid) AS outstanding
        FROM patients p2
        JOIN appointments a2 ON p2.patient_id = a2.patient_id
        JOIN invoices i2 ON a2.appointment_id = i2.appointment_id
        WHERE i2.payment_status IN ('unpaid', 'partial')
        GROUP BY p2.patient_id, p2.insurance_provider
    ) sub
    WHERE sub.insurance_provider = p.insurance_provider
)

semantic_checklist.comparison_logic: Filter lab results where test_value exceeds the average test_value for the same test_name, and the associated patient's total spending exceeds the average total spending across all patients.

generated_sql.sql:
WITH patient_spending AS (
    SELECT a.patient_id, SUM(i.total_amount) AS total_spent
    FROM appointments a
    JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
    GROUP BY a.patient_id
),
avg_patient_spending AS (
    SELECT AVG(total_spent) AS avg_spent
    FROM patient_spending
),
avg_test_value AS (
    SELECT test_name, AVG(test_value) AS avg_val
    FROM lab_results
    GROUP BY test_name
)
SELECT lr.lab_id, lr.test_name, lr.test_value, lr.result_flag, lr.result_date
FROM lab_results lr
JOIN appointments a ON lr.appointment_id = a.appointment_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
JOIN patients p ON a.patient_id = p.patient_id
JOIN patient_spending ps ON p.patient_id = ps.patient_id
JOIN avg_test_value atv ON lr.test_name = atv.test_name
CROSS JOIN avg_patient_spending aps
WHERE lr.test_value > atv.avg_val
  AND ps.total_spent > aps.avg_spent

SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name
FROM patients p

       WHERE payment_status != 'paid'

rejected_candidates.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
JOIN lab_results l ON a.appointment_id = l.appointment_id
WHERE l.result_flag IN ('high', 'low', 'critical')
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.test_name) > 1
   AND (SUM(i.total_amount) - SUM(i.insurance_paid)) > (
       SELECT AVG(total_outstanding)
       FROM (
           SELECT SUM(i2.total_amount) - SUM(i2.insurance_paid) AS total_outstanding
           FROM appointments a2
           JOIN invoices i2 ON a2.appointment_id = i2.appointment_id
           GROUP BY a2.patient_id
       )
   )
reasons: SQL uses unmentioned table 'lab_results' while the question names specific tables ['patients']
SQL uses unmentioned table 'invoices' while the question names specific tables ['patients']

grain violation [requirement 2/2]: contract requires the measure over ALL rows of the entity (lifetime/overall SUM(invoices.total_amount) per patients.patient_id), but every SUM(total_amount) used in the comparison is computed from a restricted scope: measure scope is filtered by an unrelated qualifying condition on lab_results.result_flag
possible join fanout aggregate inflation

rejected_candidates.label: llm_sql_repair
rejected_candidates.sql:
WITH patient_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        p.patient_id,
        p.patient_name,
        COUNT(DISTINCT lr.test_name) AS abnormal_test_count,
        SUM(i.total_amount) - SUM(i.insurance_paid) AS outstanding_balance
    FROM patients p
    JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
    JOIN lab_results lr ON a.appointment_id = lr.appointment_id
    JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
    WHERE lr.result_flag IN ('high', 'low', 'critical')
    GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name
),
avg_balance AS (
    SELECT AVG(outstanding_balance) AS avg_outstanding
    FROM patient_stats
)
SELECT 
    ps.patient_id,
    ps.patient_name
FROM patient_stats ps
CROSS JOIN avg_balance ab
WHERE ps.abnormal_test_count > 1
  AND ps.outstanding_balance > ab.avg_outstanding
reasons: checklist requires shape 'group_by_having' but the SQL lacks it
SQL uses unmentioned table 'lab_results' while the question names specific tables ['patients']
SQL uses unmentioned table 'invoices' while the question names specific tables ['patients']
